359	- *Mechanical* — typeclass wiring, instance synthesis, ring-level algebra, simp/ring-tactic territory, definitional unfolding glue. A prover lane can comfortably close 3–6 of these in one iter (the attempt-cap permits each one a fresh budget). When the upcoming work is mechanical, load lanes aggressively.
360	- *Deep* — the load-bearing categorical / geometric / analytic argument. One per lane, often less. The prover may also legitimately spend an iter exploring without closing anything.
361	
362	Use this partition to decide how thickly to load each lane. Don't load a deep lane with three deep sorries; that just thrashes the prover's attempt budget across all three. Don't restrict a mechanical lane to one sorry "to keep it simple"; the prover wants the batched objectives.
363	
364	Balance difficulty so all provers finish around the same time. Avoid shallow / trivial objectives unless they unblock something downstream this iter. Don't artificially throttle — the prover prompt says "push as far as possible"; your objective list must give it room to.
365	
366	If a previous experiment is being restarted, check compilation status of every target `.lean` first. Prioritize files with sorries or compile errors; don't redo completed work.
367	
368	**Dispatch cap.** The runner refuses to fan out more than ~10 provers in a single iter (configurable via `--max-objectives`, default 10). Writing 15+ files into `## Current Objectives` is a planning failure, not a tooling limitation — even when the project has many open files, the right iter-level move is to pick the most urgent ≤10 (mechanical lanes counted) and defer the rest to the next iter. If the deterministic plan-validate guard truncates your list, the surplus is added to `USER_HINTS.md` so the next planner sees what got deferred. Don't rely on the safety net — pick the right ~10 the first time.
369	
370	**Blocked-deps filter.** Before dispatch, plan-validate also drops any objective whose transitive local imports failed the *previous* `lake build`. Reason: a prover assigned to `Downstream.lean` that imports `Upstream.lean`-which-doesn't-compile would fail to even load the file, burning API time for nothing. The blocked set is parsed from `.archon/last_lake_build.log`. There is one important exception: a blocked file that's *itself* an objective this iter is presumed-being-fixed — the planner is allowed to assign `Upstream.lean` and `Downstream.lean` together (the prover phase handles them in import order). When the filter drops files, they're listed in `USER_HINTS.md` with their specific blocking deps, so you can prioritize fixing the upstream files next iter. Best practice: when you see `## Build state` flagging compile errors, put those files at the top of `## Current Objectives` so the filter exempts the downstream lanes that depend on them.
371	
372	## Dependency graph
373	
374	Optional but cheap. Before scoping objectives, you may run:
375	
376	```
377	${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/dependency_graph.py" . --format=summary
378	```
379	
380	It parses imports + chapter `\lean{...}` / `\uses{...}` / `\proves{...}` / markers and emits a project-wide view in under a second. Use it to order objectives — upstream files first, downstream files later.
381	
382	## Stage transitions
383	
384	Advance `PROGRESS.md` when all current-stage objectives are met:
385	
386	- `autoformalize` → `prover` (all statements formalized)
387	- `prover` → `polish` (all sorries filled and verified)
388	- `polish` → `COMPLETE` (proofs clean, compile)

1	# Strategy
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge (`references/challenge.lean`):
6	nine protected declarations headlined by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and
7	`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` — existence of an Albanese / Jacobian
8	object uniform over the `k`-rational pointing of a smooth proper
9	geometrically irreducible curve `C / k`, with **no** `C(k) ≠ ∅` hypothesis.
10	End-state: zero inline `sorry`, kernel-only axioms. **Char-general:** the
11	protected signatures take `[Field k]` only — NO `CharZero`. Any char-0
12	sub-case route is therefore PARTIAL only.
13	
14	The witness OBJECT `J` is always real (constructed unconditionally); only
15	`isAlbaneseFor` is universally quantified over `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`. Spine:
16	**pointed vs. unpointed**. `genus C := \dim_k H^1(C, O_C)` (arithmetic
17	genus; protected — cannot be re-typed).
18

22	EASIEST sub-phase; deeper substrate (Stacks 052H, Quot/Grassmannian,
23	codim-1) plausibly runs at ~10–25 LOC/it because each iter must first
24	invent missing Mathlib API. Velocity figures in
25	`LOC (remaining · realized/it)`. Cell-internal arithmetic: `remaining ÷
26	realized` should roughly match `Iters left`.

61	## Routes
62	
63	Positive-genus arm = **Route A (Picard scheme via FGA)** — mandatory.
64	Genus-0 arm = **Route C (Milne §I.3 rigidity)** — `J = Spec k` trivial,
65	char-general.
66	
67	### Route A — Picard scheme via FGA
68	
69	`J := Pic⁰_{C/k}` per Kleiman §4–§5 + Nitsure §5 + Milne III §6. A.2 +
70	A.4 decomposed per table. A.4.a = dominant risk; A.4.b independently
71	startable.

74	A.2.a.iii ⊳ A.2.a.ii; A.2.b.ii ⊳ A.1.a + A.2.a.i; A.2.b.iii ⊳ A.2.b.i +
75	A.2.b.ii; A.2.c ⊳ A.2.b.iii + A.1.c; A.3 ⊳ A.2.c; A.4.c ⊳ A.4.a +
76	A.4.b; A.4.d.ii ⊳ A.4.d.i + A.3 + A.4.c.
77	
78	### Route C — genus-0 rigidity via Milne §I.3
79	
80	`J = Spec k` trivial. Over `k̄`, every pointed `f : C → A` is constant.
81	Rigidity Lemma + Cor 1.5 + Cor 1.2 axiom-clean. Base case `ℙ¹→A const`
82	via 𝔾_m-scaling shortcut (chart-bridge currently axiom-laundered).
83	Genus-0 ⟹ ℙ¹ via RR bridge.

106	  asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md` per the loop's "You decide;

110	  extension**. Prove `ℙ¹ → A const` directly via: (i) extend an
111	  `𝔸¹ → A` morphism on the affine chart to all of `ℙ¹` via the

113	  criterion); (ii) the EXTENDED morphism is forced to be constant on

116	  per strategy-critic `iter178`: the valuative criterion supplies

71	### Signature mismatch (critical)
72	- `Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff` at L192: the RHS `Nonempty { s : H⁰ // s ≠ 0 }` does not bind `s` to `f` and is therefore *vacuous in `f`*; the iff degenerates to `(order conditions on f) ↔ True`, which is FALSE for any nonzero `f` with a pole at some `Q ≠ P`. Blueprint pins the substantively different statement "`f` defines a global section of `𝒪_C(P)` iff order conditions". The Lean as typed admits a counterexample (`f = f_Q^{-1}` for any closed `Q ≠ P` falsifies LHS but RHS is always true via the structural inclusion `𝒪_C ↪ 𝒪_C(P)`).

83	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
84	
85	- **Coverage**: 5/5 Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: 0 helpers + 0 substantive.
86	- **Proof-sketch depth**: **adequate**. The chapter goes into substantial proof detail for all four substantive results — LES with explicit terms for `h1_vanishing`, χ-substitution with the `RR.2`-bridge for `dim_eq_two`, lift-from-quotient for `exists_nonconstant`, and the explicit stalk-by-stalk DVR identification for `globalSections_iff`. The §1 definition section is even better, giving both the Hartshorne `ℒ(D)` subsheaf-of-`𝒦_C` construction *and* the equivalent dual-of-ideal-sheaf construction, with a paragraph on why the latter is the better Lean entry point (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData` packaging).
87	- **Hint precision**: precise on the *declaration names* (each `\lean{...}` resolves uniquely), but the prose of `lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff` leaves the *iff-binding shape* implicit. The chapter writes "`f` defines a global section iff order conditions"; the Lean reader has to infer that this binds the section to `f`. Since the Lean ended up *not* binding them, this is one place the blueprint could be tightened to forbid the vacuous reading.
88	- **Generality**: matches need. The chapter explicitly scopes itself to the `D = [P]` specialisation (cf. "Out of scope" §) and the downstream `RR.4` consumer asks for exactly that.
89	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**:
90	  - Tighten `lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff`'s informal statement so the iff explicitly binds the section to the rational function (e.g. "there exists a global section `s` of `𝒪_C(P)` whose image under the inclusion `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` equals `f`"). The prose proof already establishes this binding stalk-by-stalk; pulling the binding into the lemma statement makes the Lean signature unambiguous.
91	  - Optional: add a one-line "the inclusion `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C` defining this correspondence" gloss in the §2 introductory paragraph so the iter-181+ prover knows which Lean map plays this role (likely a `LinearMap` produced by the dual-of-ideal-sheaf construction's inclusion into the constant sheaf at `K(C)`).
92	  - Optionally, the §1 description authorising `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` to be constructed via either of the two equivalent recipes could be sharpened by recommending one — the prover's task_result notes infrastructure is missing for the `Sheaf`-internal-Hom + `ModuleCat`-forget path, so the chapter could pre-commit to the subsheaf-of-`𝒦_C` route (which avoids the `IdealSheafDual` ground-up helper file the prover currently recommends).

94	## Severity summary
95	
96	- **must-fix-this-iter**:
97	  1. `globalSections_iff` (L192): signature mismatch — the typed iff has a vacuous-in-`f` RHS and is FALSE as a mathematical claim (per the rules' "Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose" clause). The fix is to bind the existence quantifier to `f`, e.g. `∃ s, ι s = f` where `ι : H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) → K(C)` is the inclusion-into-`𝒦_C`. Until this is fixed, the proof obligation is not the one the blueprint claims, and `exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (which the blueprint sketches via this iff) cannot consume it as the chapter prose describes.
98	  2. `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` (L140): placeholder `:= sorry` body on the file's central substantive definition. Blueprint specifies two concrete recipes.

They are bodies, not signatures, and the iter cadence is making forward progress on the dual-of-ideal-sheaf infrastructure that gates them. The plan agent should treat finding 1 as the hard gate (the file ships a false-as-typed iff under sorry) and findings 2–5 as the still-outstanding pinned-sorry list this skeleton was always going to carry.

15	## RingTheory.Module.depth (line 146)
16	
17	### Attempt 1
18	- **Approach:** Stacks 00LF supremum form via
19	  `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`. Body:
20	
21	  ```lean
22	  noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
23	      (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
24	    open Classical in
25	    if _I • (⊤ : Submodule R _M) = ⊤ then (⊤ : ℕ∞)
26	    else sSup { n : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = n ∧
27	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ _I) ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular _M rs }
28	  ```
29	
30	- **Result:** RESOLVED first attempt.
31	- **Axiom hygiene:** `#print axioms RingTheory.Module.depth` → exactly
32	  `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (verified via `lean_verify`).
33	- **Key insight:** `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular L rs` in
34	  `Mathlib/RingTheory/Regular/RegularSequence.lean:146` packages the
35	  "weakly regular + `M/(rs)·M ≠ 0`" pair exactly matching Stacks 00LF.
36	  The `IM = M` (trivial-quotient) clause is forced to `⊤` via the
37	  `if`-branch, matching Stacks' "if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`".
38	  `open Classical in` supplies the decidability instance for the
39	  submodule equality test (`noncomputable def` already in force).
40	
41	### Notes on semantics
42	
43	- For `M = 0` (or `I = R`): `_I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤` holds, body
44	  returns `⊤`. Matches Stacks convention `depth(0) = ∞`.
45	- For `M ≠ 0` with `_I • ⊤ ≠ ⊤`: body is the sSup of the set of
46	  lengths of regular sequences in `I`, valued in `ℕ∞ = WithTop ℕ`.
47	  `sSup ∅ = 0` (when no regular sequence in `I` exists), matching the
48	  ground-truth depth-0 case (every element of `I` is a zero-divisor on
49	  `M`). `IsRegular L []` requires `[Nontrivial L]`, so on a trivial
50	  module the empty list is not regular — but we already branched to
51	  `⊤` above in that case.
52	
53	## Depth-dependent sorries (lines 228, 268, 326, 396) — off-target per Lane H
54	
55	Per the iter-180 Lane H "Off-target" clause:
56	> All other sorries in `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (depth-dependent
57	> lemmas): leave; iter-181+ work after `depth` body lands.

59	The 4 remaining sorries:
60	
61	1. `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (L228) — Stacks 00LP. Body sketch in
62	   docstring: induction on `n` via long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` on
63	   `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`. Requires choosing a non-zero-divisor
64	   `x ∈ 𝔪`, the `M`-side `IsSMulRegular` characterization, and
65	   `Ext`-vanishing-below-`n` ↔ depth-bound `n ≤ depth(M)`.
66	2. `depth_of_short_exact` (L268) — Stacks 00LE three inequalities;
67	   chases the long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` of `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0`
68	   combined with `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`.
69	3. `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` (L326) — Stacks 090V. Induction on
70	   `depth(M)`: base via minimal finite free resolution + "what is
71	   exact" + iterated `depth_of_short_exact`; inductive step via
72	   snake lemma on multiplication-by-`x` and IH on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
73	4. `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` (L396) — Stacks 00OD. Direct
74	   regular-sequence argument: minimal generating set of `𝔪` of size
75	   `d = dim R` is `R`-regular (uses `R` is a domain via Stacks 00NQ)
76	   plus the upper bound `depth ≤ dim` (Stacks 00LK).

74	```lean
75	indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
76	  ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
77	    ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left)
78	```
79	
80	This says: `Z(f)` is empty OR each x ∈ Z(f) lies in the closure of some
81	codim-1 point z. The second disjunct does NOT preclude a codim-1 point
82	z from being in Z(f) — in fact z ∈ closure({z}) trivially, so a
83	codim-1 generic point inside Z(f) satisfies the witness role itself.

86	codim-1 point in Z(f)) without an additional input ruling out codim-1
87	points in Z(f). That input IS the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1.

159	/-! ## §2. Global sections as the Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`
160	
161	Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7 identifies the global sections of
162	`ℒ(D_0)` with the rational functions `f ∈ K(X)^×` satisfying
163	`div(f) ≥ −D_0` (plus the zero section). Specialised to `D_0 = [P]` on a
164	curve, the condition `div(f) + [P] ≥ 0` rewrites coordinate-wise as
165	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`. -/
166	
167	/-- **Global sections of `𝒪_C(P)` as rational functions with controlled
168	pole at `P`** (Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7, p. 157).
169	
170	For a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×`, the following are
171	equivalent:
172	
173	* `f` defines a global section of `𝒪_C(P)` (equivalently: there exists
174	  a nonzero `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under the canonical
175	  inclusion `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` is `f`);
176	* the order conditions hold: `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
177	  `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose generic point is not `P`, and
178	  `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (where the latter is read off the prime divisor
179	  `⟨P, h⟩` with `h : Order.coheight P = 1` the codimension-one witness
180	  automatic for a closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme).

182	The iff is the substantive content of Hartshorne's Proposition 7.7(b) /
183	its proof, specialised to `D_0 = [P]`.

307	of a non-zero element of the quotient is, under the identification of
308	`globalSections_iff`, a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with
309	the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
310	This is the seed of `RR.4` (the morphism `C → ℙ¹` produced by
311	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` from the basis `(1, f)`). -/
312	
313	/-- **Existence of a non-constant rational function regular on `C ∖ {P}`
314	with at most a simple pole at `P`** (Hartshorne IV §1 Exercise 1.1,
315	p. 297, the genus-`0` specialisation).
316	
317	Concretely, there exists `f ∈ K(C)` such that:
318	
319	* `f ≠ 0`;
320	* `f ∉ k̄` (i.e. `f` is non-constant — for instance, it does not lie
321	  in the image of the structural inclusion of constants);
322	* `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose
323	  generic point is not `P`;
324	* `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
325	
326	iter-177+ body: use `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` to get
327	`dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. The image of `1 ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) ≅ k̄`
328	under the structural inclusion `𝒪_C ↪ 𝒪_C(P)` spans a one-dimensional
329	subspace of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))`; choose any element of the complement
330	(non-empty because `dim H⁰ = 2 > 1`), lift it through
331	`globalSections_iff` to a rational function `f`, and verify that the
332	three bullets hold. The lift is non-constant because it is not in the
333	constant subspace.
334	
335	The principal-divisor-non-zero formulation `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
336	f hf ≠ 0` follows from non-constancy plus the fact that constant
337	functions have principal divisor zero (the converse — `div(f) = 0`
338	⇒ `f` constant — uses the integrality of `C` and is the Stacks 02P0
339	"functions with no zeros and poles are constant" type statement).
340	
341	Blueprint reference:
342	`cor:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (alias
343	`cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero` consumed by `RR.4`)
344	(Hartshorne IV.1 Exercise 1.1, p. 297). -/
345	theorem exists_nonconstant_genusZero
346	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
347	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
348	    (P : C.left) (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
349	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
350	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
351	    ∃ (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0),
352	      (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
353	          0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
354	      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f ∧
355	      Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal (X := C.left) f hf ≠ 0 := by
356	  sorry
357	
358	end lineBundleAtClosedPoint
359
Good — now I know the OCofP signature precisely. Let me check `archon-protected.yaml` to confirm `globalSections_iff` is unprotected, then start dispatching critics.

# refactor directive (iter-181, slug: ocofp-globalsections-sig)

## Background

The iter-180 review-phase `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-iter180-ocofp` returned a **CRITICAL must-fix-this-iter** finding: the Lean signature of `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff` at `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean:192` is mathematically **false as typed**.

The current RHS,

```
Nonempty
  { s : Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP) 0 //
    s ≠ 0 }
```

does not bind `s` to `f`. Since the structural inclusion `𝒪_C ↪ 𝒪_C(P)` always provides a nonzero global section (the image of `1`), the RHS is *always* `True`. So the iff degenerates to `(order conditions on f) ↔ True`, which is false for any `f` with a pole at some `Q ≠ P` (e.g. `f = f_Q^{-1}`).

The intended statement (per blueprint chapter `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_OCofP.tex` §2 and Hartshorne II.7.7) is: there exists a global section `s` of `𝒪_C(P)` whose image under the inclusion `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` equals `f`. We need a `Σ`-shape that binds `s` to `f`.

`Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff` is **not** in `archon-protected.yaml` — only the `Jacobian.*`, `Genus.genus`, `AbelJacobi.*` declarations are protected. The refactor is permitted.

## Scope of this refactor

You execute only the **signature** change + insert `sorry` for the body. The plan agent has separately scheduled an iter-181 prover lane to fill the body via the iter-176 lineBundleAtClosedPoint infrastructure programme.

## Concrete change

In `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean`:

1. **Add a section-to-function map.** Above `globalSections_iff` (i.e. between the `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` definition at L140 and the `globalSections_iff` lemma at L192), introduce a **typed `:= sorry` placeholder** for the inclusion

   ```lean
   /-- The inclusion `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` of global sections of
   `𝒪_C(P)` into the function field, viewing each section as a rational
   function via the canonical embedding `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C` (Hartshorne II §6 p.
   144).  The body lands together with the body of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`. -/
   noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField
       {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
       {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
       [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
       [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
       (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
       (s : Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP) 0) :
       C.left.functionField :=
     sorry
   ```

   The exact shape matters: the section produced should be a `k̄`-linear map (it's the global-section "evaluation at the generic point" plus the inclusion of the structure sheaf into the function field), so the bare `def` going from `HModule … 0` to `functionField` is correct. Bodies land later — keep `sorry`.

2. **Re-type the iff.** Replace the existing iff body with the binding shape. The new statement is:

   ```lean
   lemma globalSections_iff
       [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
           Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
       (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
       (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
       (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
       (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
           0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
         (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f
       ↔
       ∃ s : Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP) 0,
         lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField (C := C) P hP s = f := by
     sorry
   ```

   (The previous body had a `refine ⟨fun _h => ?_, fun _h => ?_⟩` skeleton; delete it and leave a single `sorry`.)

## Mode

api-alignment

## Background

`AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean` carries 2 long-deferred Pins (Pin 2, Pin 3) that gate the genus-0 RR.4 chain (rational curve over `k̄` ⟹ isomorphic to `ℙ¹`) and downstream `WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` (non-constant branch). The pins have been STUCK by deferral across iter-179 + iter-180 (no consult dispatched, no in-project alternative attempted). I need to know whether Mathlib has either of these pieces, or a near-equivalent the project can consume directly.

## The two pins

### Pin 2 — `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`

**Mathematical content**: For a non-constant morphism `f : C → ℙ¹` between smooth proper curves over an algebraically closed field, the **degree of `f`** (in the sense of `deg(f) := [K(C) : f^*K(ℙ¹)]`, the field extension degree) equals the **degree of the pole divisor `f^*∞`** (the pullback of the closed point `∞ ∈ ℙ¹`, taken as a Weil divisor on `C`).

This is **Hartshorne II.6 Proposition 6.9** (also II.6.9.1), specialised to the codomain `ℙ¹`. Equivalent / nearby Stacks: 0BE5, 0BCM, 0AYL.

**Project-side type signature** (in `RationalCurveIso.lean`): the Lean lemma asserts `Scheme.degree f = (Scheme.WeilDivisor.pullback f ∞).degree` (or similar — please verify by reading the file).

**What I need from you**:
1. Search Mathlib (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/...`) for any of:
   - `Scheme.Hom.degree`, `Morphism.degree`, `FunctionField.transcendenceDegree`, `RingHom.localizationDegree`
   - the multiplicativity-of-degree formula
   - "ramification" / "branched cover" combinatorial degree
   - Hartshorne II.6.9 Lean port
2. Verify: does Mathlib currently expose any of (a) `Scheme.degree` for a morphism between smooth proper curves over a field, (b) the pullback of a Weil divisor `f^*D`, (c) the equality of degrees?
3. If yes → name the exact declaration(s) and how the project's Lean signature should be re-aligned (or expressed as a thin wrapper).
4. If no → name the closest Mathlib infrastructure that exists and would let the project build the degree map / multiplicativity in ~50–150 LOC. Is `RingTheory.Ideal.Norm` / `FractionalIdeal.norm` close enough?

### Pin 3 — `iso_of_degree_one`

**Mathematical content**: A morphism `f : C → ℙ¹` between smooth proper curves over an algebraically closed field with **`deg(f) = 1`** is an **isomorphism**. This is **Stacks tag 0AVX** (or the equivalent Hartshorne IV.2 — birational morphism of complete curves is iso). 

Equivalent formulations: (a) `deg(f) = 1 ⟹ [K(C) : K(ℙ¹)] = 1 ⟹ f^* : K(ℙ¹) → K(C)` is an iso of fields ⟹ `f` is birational ⟹ since `C` and `ℙ¹` are smooth proper, `f` is an iso (Zariski's main theorem / Hartshorne III.11.4 for the projective case).

**Project-side type signature** (in `RationalCurveIso.lean`): roughly `f : C ⟶ ℙ¹ → deg f = 1 → IsIso f`.

**What I need from you**:
1. Search Mathlib for any of:
   - `Scheme.Hom.isIso_of_deg_one`, `Morphism.isIso_of_birational`
   - `IsBirational ⟹ IsIso` for smooth proper schemes / curves
   - Zariski's main theorem (Mathlib has `ZariskiMainTheorem`?)
   - `Hartshorne.III_11_4` or analogues
2. Verify: does Mathlib expose any of (a) `IsBirational` for scheme morphisms, (b) the smooth-proper-curve "birational ⟹ iso" lemma, (c) the closed-immersion-via-degree-1 path?
3. If yes → name the lemma(s) + how the project's signature aligns.
4. If no → name the closest infrastructure (e.g. `Quasi-finite + proper + birational ⟹ iso` should exist in Mathlib in some form even if not specialised to curves) and a ~50–100 LOC strategy.

## Output format

Per pin: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB (Mathlib has it, project must use it) / PROCEED (project's own bespoke build is acceptable; no Mathlib alternative) / GAP (Mathlib doesn't have it but the closest infra needs ~K LOC; recommend project build). Include verbatim Mathlib decl names + file paths when available.

If both pins return GAP, the iter-182 planner has to make a "axiomatise" vs "in-tree build" decision and your report frames that decision.

Persistent file: write recipes to `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md` (Pin 2) and `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` (Pin 3). Report under `task_results/mathlib-analogist-ratcurveiso-pins.md`.

`task_results/**`, `analogies/**`.

1	\chapter{Milne Theorem 3.2: Rational maps into abelian varieties}
2	\label{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-174). This chapter is the A.4.c sub-row of Route A
6	% (the positive-genus arm of \texttt{nonempty\_jacobianWitness}). It is the
7	% join of the two preceding sub-rows: A.4.a, the codim-1 indeterminacy /
8	% codim-$\geq 2$ extension package (\cref{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension}),
9	% and A.4.b, the Auslander--Buchsbaum corollary
10	% ``regular local $\Rightarrow$ Cohen--Macaulay''
11	% (\cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}). The single output of this
12	% chapter is Milne's Theorem~3.2 of Chapter~I~\S 3 of \emph{Abelian Varieties}:
13	% \emph{every rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety
14	% extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism}. This is the input that A.4.d
15	% (\cref{chap:Albanese_AlbaneseUP}) consumes to upgrade the symmetric-product
16	% rational map $C^{(g)} \dashrightarrow A$ to a regular morphism $J \to A$,
17	% which in turn yields the Albanese universal property of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$
18	% (Milne III~\S 6, Proposition~6.1).
19	%
20	% This chapter is also the canonical home of the Lean target
21	% \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend\_to\_av}. A placeholder
22	% lemma block \texttt{lem:rational\_map\_to\_av\_extends} was reserved
23	% earlier in \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} (under the alternative Lean
24	% name \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.rationalMap\_to\_av\_extends}); a Plan-Agent
25	% reconciliation pass should retarget that reservation at the theorem proved
26	% here (see "Notes for Plan Agent" in the writer report).
27	%
28	% The Lean target file \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean}
29	% does not yet exist; this chapter is the prover-ready specification for
30	% creating it.
31	
32	\section{Setup and statement}
33	\label{sec:thm32_setup}
34	
35	Fix an algebraically closed field $\bar k$, a smooth variety $X$ over $\bar k$ (in the
36	project's convention: a geometrically integral, geometrically reduced, separated scheme of
37	finite type over $\bar k$ that is moreover smooth, hence nonsingular at every closed point),
38	and an abelian variety $A$ over $\bar k$ (in particular $A$ is complete, smooth, and a
39	group object in $\mathrm{Sch}/\bar k$). A rational map
40	$f \colon X \dashrightarrow A$ is, in the project's notation, the equivalence class of a
41	regular morphism $\varphi_U \colon U \to A$ defined on a dense open subset
42	$U \subseteq X$, two such pairs $(U, \varphi_U)$ and $(U', \varphi_{U'})$ being equivalent
43	if $\varphi_U$ and $\varphi_{U'}$ agree on $U \cap U'$.
44	
45	The result of this chapter is:
46	
47	\begin{theorem}
48	
49	\leanok
50	[Milne Theorem~3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends]
51	  \label{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends}
52	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av}
53	  \uses{thm:codim_one_extension, thm:auslander_buchsbaum, cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
54	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.2, \S I.3, doc p.~17
55	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 23).
56	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
57	  % "THEOREM 3.2. A rational map $\alpha\colon V--\to A$ from a nonsingular
58	  %  variety to an abelian variety is defined on the whole of $V$."
59	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.2, \S I.3, p.~17.}
60

60	
61	  Let $X$ be a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$, let $A$ be
62	  an abelian variety over $\bar k$, and let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow A$ be a rational
63	  map. Then $f$ is defined on the whole of $X$: there is a unique regular morphism
64	  $\widetilde f \colon X \to A$ whose restriction to some (equivalently, every) dense open
65	  set of definition $U \subseteq X$ of $f$ agrees with $\varphi_U$.
66	\end{theorem}
67	
68	The uniqueness clause is immediate from $X$ being reduced and separated: two regular
69	morphisms $X \to A$ that agree on a dense open subset agree everywhere. The substance is
70	existence of the extension. Milne deduces it in one line by combining the codim-$\geq 2$
71	extension result for rational maps to a complete variety with the codim-1 indeterminacy
72	structure result for rational maps to a group variety:
73	
74	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
75	% "PROOF. Combine Theorem 3.1 with the next lemma."
76	\begin{proof}
77	  \uses{thm:codim_one_extension, thm:auslander_buchsbaum, cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
78	  \leanok
79	  Milne's one-line proof is: \emph{combine Theorem~3.1 with Lemma~3.3}.
80	  Theorem~3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension theorem for a rational map from a normal
81	  variety to a complete variety; valuative criterion of properness) is
82	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} of the present project; Lemma~3.3 (the
83	  pure-codimension-$1$ structure of the indeterminacy locus of a rational map into a
84	  group variety) is \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} of the same chapter.
85	
86	  Concretely: let $U \subseteq X$ be a maximal open set of definition of $f$, and let
87	  $Z := X \setminus U$ be its complement.
88	  \begin{enumerate}
89	    \item By \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} (Milne~3.1, the valuative-criterion extension
90	      to codim $\geq 2$), applied with the normal variety $X$ and the complete variety
91	      $A$, we have $\mathrm{codim}_X(Z) \geq 2$.
92	    \item By \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} (Milne~3.3, the pure-codim-$1$
93	      indeterminacy structure for maps into the group variety $A$), the closed subset
94	      $Z$ is either empty or of pure codimension $1$ in $X$.
95	    \item Both conditions together force $Z = \emptyset$: a non-empty pure-codimension-$1$
96	      closed subset has codimension exactly $1$, contradicting $\mathrm{codim}_X(Z) \geq 2$.
97	  \end{enumerate}
98	  Therefore $U = X$ and $f$ admits a regular representative on all of $X$, which is the
99	  required regular morphism $\widetilde f \colon X \to A$. Uniqueness is the standard
100	  reduced-and-separated agreement principle (see the paragraph preceding the theorem).
101	\end{proof}
102	
103	\begin{remark}
104	  \label{rmk:thm32_role_of_ab}
105	  \textbf{Where Auslander--Buchsbaum enters.} The combination above relies on
106	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}, which is itself proved in
107	  \cref{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension} in two stages: a codim-$1$ step
108	  (valuative criterion of properness, applied at the DVR
109	  $\mathcal O_{X, Z}$ of each codimension-$1$ point $Z$ --- works because $X$ is normal
110	  and $A$ is complete) and a codim-$\geq 2$ step (depth-$\geq 2$ at codim-$\geq 2$ points,
111	  giving the local-cohomology / Hartshorne~III.8 sheaf-extension property). The
112	  depth-$\geq 2$ input on a smooth (regular) variety is exactly what
113	  \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} of \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} supplies:
114	  for a regular local ring $(\mathcal O_{X, x}, \mathfrak m_x)$ of Krull dimension $d$, the
115	  Auslander--Buchsbaum formula \cref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} (applied to $M = R$
116	  trivially, or to the depth-via-regular-sequence characterisation directly) gives
117	  $\mathrm{depth}(\mathcal O_{X, x}) = d$, so in particular
118	  $\mathrm{depth}(\mathcal O_{X, x}) \geq 2$ whenever $\mathrm{codim}_X(\{x\}) \geq 2$.
119	  Concretely, when $X$ is a smooth surface (e.g.\ $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$ or
120	  $C \times C$ for a smooth projective curve $C$), every closed point $x$ is regular of
121	  dimension $2$, so $\mathcal O_{X, x}$ is Cohen--Macaulay of depth $2$; sections of
122	  $\mathcal O_X$ on $U = X \setminus \{x\}$ extend uniquely across $x$, and the same is
123	  true for affine-locally pulled-back sections of $\mathcal O_A$ along $f$.
124	
125	  Auslander--Buchsbaum therefore does not appear by name in the proof above; it appears
126	  inside \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s codim-$\geq 2$ half. The dependency
127	  $\uses{thm:auslander_buchsbaum, cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}$ is recorded on
128	  \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends} to keep the leanblueprint dependency graph
129	  faithful: A.4.c truly does sit on top of A.4.b through A.4.a.
130	\end{remark}
131	
132	\section{Why the proof is char-free}
133	\label{sec:thm32_char_free}
134	
135	Each of the two inputs is char-free:
136	\begin{itemize}
137	  \item \emph{Theorem~3.1 / codim-$\geq 2$ extension} (\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}):
138	    the proof uses only the valuative criterion of properness and depth-$\geq 2$ extension
139	    of sections on regular local rings; both are characteristic-agnostic.
140	  \item \emph{Lemma~3.3 / pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy for maps into a group variety}
141	    (\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}): Milne's proof forms the difference map
142	    $\Phi(x, y) := \varphi(x)\,\varphi(y)^{-1} \colon X \times X \dashrightarrow A$ and
143	    examines its zero/pole divisor along the diagonal --- a Weil-divisor argument that
144	    works for any algebraically closed base field. The argument uses only the group law
145	    on $A$ (its smoothness as a group object), the regularity of $X$ (so divisors and
146	    DVRs at codim-$1$ points are available), and the existence of a divisor of zeros and
147	    poles for a nonzero rational function on a nonsingular variety. None of these inputs
148	    has a characteristic restriction.
149	\end{itemize}
150	Consequently \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends} holds over every algebraically closed
151	field, in arbitrary characteristic. (Milne states this proof verbatim in characteristic-free
152	generality at \S I.3, pp.~16--17.)
153	
154	\section{Application: the symmetric-product Albanese map (gating A.4.d)}
155	\label{sec:thm32_application_to_a4d}
156	
157	The downstream consumer is A.4.d (\cref{chap:Albanese_AlbaneseUP}):
158	the Albanese universal property of the Jacobian $J = \Pic^0_{C/k}$. Milne's proof of
159	Proposition~6.1 of \S III.6 (\emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~104) constructs the unique
160	morphism $\psi \colon J \to A$ factoring a given pointed regular morphism
161	$\varphi \colon C \to A$ through the canonical $f_P \colon C \to J$ as follows. The
162	symmetric power $C^{(g)}$ admits a regular morphism $\varphi^{(g)} \colon C^{(g)} \to A$
163	defined by
164	\[
165	  \varphi^{(g)}\bigl(P_1 + \dots + P_g\bigr) \;=\; \varphi(P_1) + \dots + \varphi(P_g),
166	\]
167	and a birational morphism $C^{(g)} \dashrightarrow J$ (the Abel--Jacobi map). One then
168	seeks a morphism $J \to A$ making the obvious diagram commute. The construction goes via
169	$C^{(g)} \dashrightarrow A$ followed by descent to $J$; the rational map

1	# Mathlib Analogist Report
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	ratcurveiso-pins
8	
9	## Iteration
10	181
11	
12	## Question
13	
14	For the two long-deferred Pins in
15	`AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean`:
16	- **Pin 2** (`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`, Hartshorne II.6.9):
17	  does Mathlib expose a `Scheme.Hom.degree`, a Weil-divisor pullback,
18	  or the multiplicativity-of-degree formula?
19	- **Pin 3** (`iso_of_degree_one`, Hartshorne I.6.12 / Stacks 0AVX):
20	  does Mathlib expose `IsBirational`, Zariski's main theorem, or the
21	  "degree-1 ⟹ iso for smooth proper curves" specialisation?
22	
23	If yes → the project should align with the Mathlib idiom. If no →
24	recommend the closest infrastructure + ~K LOC strategy.
25	
26	## Verdicts (summary)
27	
28	| Decision | Verdict | Severity |
29	|---|---|---|
30	| Pin 2.1 — `Scheme.Hom.degree` for scheme morphisms | PROCEED | informational |
31	| Pin 2.2 — Weil-divisor pullback `f^* : Div(Y) → Div(X)` | PROCEED | informational |
32	| Pin 2.3 — multiplicativity-of-degree (Hartshorne II.6.9) | PROCEED | informational |
33	| Pin 3.1 — `IsBirational` / birational ⟹ iso for smooth proper curves | PROCEED | informational |
34	| Pin 3.2 — signature shape (function-field-iso hypothesis) | DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY | informational |
35	
36	**No ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB verdict.** Mathlib has no `Scheme.Hom.degree`,
37	no `Scheme.WeilDivisor`, no `Scheme.WeilDivisor.pullback`, no
38	`IsBirational`, no "degree-1 ⟹ iso" for smooth proper curves. Both
39	pins are genuine gaps in Mathlib's algebraic-geometry coverage; the
40	project's in-tree path is the correct call.
41	
42	## Informational
43	
44	### Pin 2 — `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`: PROCEED
45	
46	Mathlib has the **field-theoretic atom** for the eventual body but no
47	scheme-level wrapper:
48	
49	- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField` — exposes
50	  `Scheme.functionField` (`CommRingCat`-valued) and
51	  `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk x) X.functionField` for integral
52	  `X`. So `[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)]` is expressible as
53	  `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField`.
54	- `Mathlib.NumberTheory.RamificationInertia.Basic.Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`
55	  is the algebraic incarnation of Hartshorne II.6.9 specialised to
56	  Dedekind bases — the eventual body of Pin 2 routes
57	  the pole-divisor calculation through this lemma on each affine
58	  chart of `C'`.
59	- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Finite.IsFinite` and
60	  `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom` give the finite-morphism
61	  predicate the body needs.
62	- The complex-analytic `Mathlib.Analysis.Meromorphic.Divisor` and the
63	  affine-ring `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup.CommRing.Pic` are NOT
64	  applicable to the scheme-level pole-divisor calculation.
65	
66	**Body strategy** (iter-178+, ~80-150 LOC):
67	1. Introduce a private `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor` (or a
68	   `Scheme.WeilDivisor.pullback` general construction) inside
69	   `RationalCurveIso.lean` or `WeilDivisor.lean`.
70	2. Identify `d := Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField` as
71	   the degree.
72	3. Cover `C'` by affines `Spec A` containing `∞`; the preimage
73	   `Spec B` is finite Dedekind-over-Dedekind; apply
74	   `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` to write the local degree as
75	   `Σ_{Q over P_∞} e(Q|P_∞) · f(Q|P_∞) = d`.
76	
77	Verdict rationale: PROCEED because no Mathlib API exists to align
78	with; the project must build the scheme-level wrapper. The current
79	signature (existence of positive-degree Weil divisor) is the right
80	substantive content given the project-bespoke `Scheme.WeilDivisor`.
81	
82	Persistent file: `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md`.
83	
84	### Pin 3 — `iso_of_degree_one`: PROCEED + DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY
85	
86	Mathlib has **partial isomorphism criteria** but no curve-specific
87	"birational ⟹ iso":
88	
89	- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Normalization.Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`
90	  (QuasiCompact + QuasiSeparated + IsIntegralHom ⟹
91	  `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization f` is iso). Bridge to Pin 3: smooth
92	  proper curve ⟹ source `C` is normal ⟹
93	  `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization φ : C ⟶ normalization φ` is iso under
94	  the integral-hom hypothesis. Then identify `normalization φ = C'`
95	  via smoothness of `C'`.
96	- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion.isIso_iff_stalk_iso` and
97	  `isIso_iff_isIso_stalkMap` give the cleanest *reduction* to a
98	  stalk + topological iso check.
99	- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.IsIso.isIso_iff_isOpenImmersion_and_surjective`
100	  is an alternative criterion when "open immersion + surjective" is
101	  easier to certify.
102	- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.RationalMap.Scheme.RationalMap.ofFunctionField`
103	  / `.fromFunctionField` give the rational-map side of the
104	  "function-field iso ↔ birational map" correspondence, but the
105	  *extension of a rational map to a morphism* (needed to lift an
106	  abstract function-field iso back to a scheme iso) is absent for
107	  the smooth-proper-curve setting.
108	
109	**Body strategy** (iter-178+, ~80-150 LOC):
110	1. **Refine the hypothesis** of `iso_of_degree_one` from "abstract
111	   function-field iso" to "function-field map induced by `φ` is iso"
112	   (or equivalently `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField
113	   = 1`). The current existence hypothesis is a file-skeleton
114	   placeholder that is strictly weaker than what the
115	   birational-extension argument needs.
116	2. Reduce to `φ` finite via
117	   `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom`.
118	3. Apply `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` to
119	   get the normalisation iso.
120	4. Identify `Scheme.Hom.normalization φ = C'` via smoothness of
121	   `C'` (smooth proper curve ⟹ regular ⟹ normal).
122	
123	Alternative route (sheaf-cohomology, conceptually cleaner but more
124	API): show `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is iso of coherent rank-1 torsion-free
125	sheaves on a smooth proper Dedekind base.
126	
127	Pin 3.2 (signature shape) lands **DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY** because the
128	file-skeleton hypothesis was deliberately a weak placeholder; the
129	iter-178+ body work will need to strengthen it.
130	
131	Persistent file: `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`.
132	
133	## Persistent files
134	
135	- `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md` — design rationale + Mathlib
136	  precedents + body strategy for Pin 2.
137	- `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` — design rationale + Mathlib
138	  precedents + body strategy for Pin 3.
139	
140	## Overall verdict
141	
142	Both pins are **PROCEED** (in-tree project work; Mathlib has the
143	field-theoretic / normalisation atoms but no scheme-level wrappers to
144	align with). The iter-182 planner is unblocked: no axiomatise vs.
145	in-tree decision is forced — the in-tree route is the correct call.
146	The only material change for iter-178+ body work is Pin 3.2's
147	signature refinement (strengthen the function-field-iso hypothesis to
148	"induced by `φ`" or to a `Module.finrank = 1` statement).
149

Both pins PROCEED — Mathlib has atoms (`Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` for Pin 2, `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` for Pin 3) but no scheme-level wrappers. Pin 3 needs a signature refinement before bodies can be attempted (DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY). content:
186	\begin{lemma}
187	
188	
189	\leanok
190	[Global sections of $\mathcal O_C(P)$ as rational functions with a
191	controlled pole]
192	  \label{lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff}
193	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff}
194	  \uses{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint, def:order_at_point,
195	        def:principal_divisor, def:divisor_closed_point}
196	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.7, Proposition~7.7, p.~157 (effective
197	  % divisors linearly equivalent to $D_0$ are the divisors of zeros of
198	  % global sections of $\mathscr L(D_0)$, with the explicit
199	  % identification of those global sections with rational functions
200	  % $f$ satisfying $(f) \geq -D_0$) (read from
201	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 174).
202	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Proposition 7.7. \emph{Let $X$ be a nonsingular
203	  % projective variety over the algebraically closed field $k$. Let
204	  % $D_0$ be a divisor on $X$ and let $\mathscr L = \mathscr L(D_0)$
205	  % be the corresponding invertible sheaf. Then:

# Iter-181 prover objectives (detailed)

## Critic-disclosure 3-tier vocabulary (applies to all lanes)

Every closed lemma in your task_result MUST classify the body as one of:

1. **kernel-clean (this body)** — `#print axioms <lemma>` returns
   `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (the kernel baseline only).
2. **kernel-clean modulo upstream `<X>` / `<Y>`** — local body has no
   `sorry`, but `#print axioms <lemma>` returns `…, sorryAx, …` because
   the transitive dependency `<X>` or `<Y>` is sorry-bodied. List the
   named upstream sorry carriers explicitly.
3. **kernel-clean (transitively)** — `#print axioms <lemma>` returns
   the kernel baseline AND every transitive dependency is sorry-free.

NEVER report a body as "axiom-clean" without checking `#print axioms`
on it. The iter-180 lean-auditor MAJOR was triggered by exactly this
laxity (Lane E claimed "axiom-clean" but inherited `sorryAx` via
upstream RR.2). Use the 3-tier vocabulary so the reviewer can read the
status from the task_result without re-running `lean_verify` itself.

---

## Lane A — `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean`

**Target**: Close the body of `Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff`
(line ~192 post plan-phase refactor; signature `∃ s, lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField P hP s = f`).

**Background**: The plan-phase `refactor ocofp-globalsections-sig`
mutated the signature so the iff binds the global section `s` to the
rational function `f`. This Lane A fills the body. Both directions are
documented inside the existing `globalSections_iff` body (forward =
Hartshorne II.7.7(b); backward = II.7.7(a)).

**Two sub-cases (both directions)**:

- **Forward (II.7.7(b))**: given the order conditions on `f`, construct
  `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` with `toFunctionField P hP s = f`. Concretely:
  the rational function `f` (with at most a simple pole at `P` and
  holomorphic at every other prime divisor) lifts to a Cartier section
  of the locally-free sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`; `s` is the global section it
  defines (Hartshorne II.7.7(b)'s construction).
- **Backward (II.7.7(a))**: given `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` with
  `toFunctionField P hP s = f`, read off the order conditions
  stalk-by-stalk via the DVR identification
  `𝒪_C(P)_Q = 𝒪_{C,Q}` for `Q ≠ P` and
  `𝒪_C(P)_P = f_P⁻¹ · 𝒪_{C,P}`.

**Blocker**: BOTH directions consume the body of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
(L140), which is still a typed `sorry`. If you cannot fire either
direction without that body, your lane is correctly PARTIAL — leave the
forward + backward sub-cases as named honest sorries (one per direction,
with detailed comments citing the missing infrastructure piece) and
move on. Do NOT introduce a placeholder body for `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
itself — that path was already analysed iter-180 (Lane D task_result) as
blocked on Sheaf-internal-Hom + ModuleCat-forget Mathlib gaps.

**Helper budget**: 2 (one per direction if needed; both must be honest
substantive helpers with `#print axioms` reports).

**Blueprint**: `chapters/RiemannRoch_OCofP.tex` (just tightened
plan-phase to bind `s` to `f` explicitly). Read
`lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff` block + its proof
block; Hartshorne pp.~156–158 quoted verbatim.

**Verification**: BEFORE starting, verify the file compiles GREEN with
the new signature shape (i.e. the plan-phase refactor landed). If the
refactor did NOT land (e.g. signature is still vacuous-in-`f`), STOP
and report PARTIAL with "blocked on plan-phase refactor".

---

## Lane B — `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`

**Target**: Close the CROSS case of `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`
(L289 — single sorry inside the lemma body, currently the only sub-case
not handled by the diagonal `fst_eq_snd_of_mono_eq` route).

**Concrete content**: The cross case `(x, y) = (0, 1)` or `(1, 0)` in
